---
title: 'Add a circle layer'
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/"
)
```

## Adding circle layers

First, set up the base map with our data.

```{r base-map}
library(toro)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(spData)

elevation_data <- spData::nz_height |>
  sf::st_transform(4326) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    info = paste("Elevation:", format(elevation, big.mark = ","))
  )
```

### Basic example

```{r basic-circle, results='asis'}
map() |>
  set_bounds(elevation_data, padding = 100) |>
  add_circle_layer(
    id = "elevation",
    source = elevation_data,
    hover_column = "info"
  )
```

### Advanced example

```{r advanced-circle, results='asis'}
map() |>
  set_bounds(elevation_data, padding = 100) |>
  add_circle_layer(
    id = "elevation",
    source = elevation_data,
    hover_column = "info",
    paint = get_paint_options(
      "circle",
      options = list(
        colour = get_column_steps(
          "elevation",
          c(3000, 3500),
          c("#093d4a", "#11a366", "#dbe811")
        ),
        circle_radius = get_column_steps(
          "elevation",
          c(3000, 3500),
          c(2, 3, 4)
        )
      )
    ),
    layout = get_layout_options(
      "circle",
      options = list(
        "circle-sort-key" = get_column_steps(
          "elevation",
          c(3000, 3500),
          c(1, 2, 3)
        )
      )
    )
  )
```
